title: Verbatim #1: First Clinical Encounter
description: Write a verbatim account of your first supervised patient encounter. Include pre-encounter feelings, verbatim dialogue, and a theological reflection of 150-200 words. See the Verbatim Template in Materials.

title: Theology of Presence - Reflection Essay
description: In 400-500 words, articulate your theology of presence. What does it mean to 'be with' a patient who is suffering? Ground your reflection in at least one text from Module 1 reading list (Nouwen, Thurman, or APC standards).

title: Module 2 Quiz: Active Listening Skills
description: What is the difference between active listening and passive hearing in a chaplaincy context? Give one clinical instance of each and explain why the distinction matters.

title: Verbatim #2: Active Listening in Practice
description: Submit a verbatim from a patient encounter in which you practiced a specific active listening technique (open question, reflective statement, or intentional silence). Annotate each technique used in the margin notes column.

title: Interfaith Competency: Research Brief
description: Select one faith tradition other than your own (e.g., Islam, Judaism, Buddhism, Hinduism) and write a 300-word brief on death and dying practices relevant to inpatient chaplaincy. Cite at least two sources.

title: Self-Care Plan: Compassion Fatigue Prevention
description: Draft a personal self-care plan addressing the three domains: physical, relational, and spiritual. Include at least two specific practices per domain and one concrete boundary you will set in clinical work. Minimum 500 words.

title: Mid-Unit Competency Portfolio - Unit 1
description: Compile your Unit 1 competency portfolio: two verbatims with supervisor feedback incorporated, the interfaith brief, the self-care plan, and a 200-word integrative reflection on your growth. Submit as a single PDF.

title: Verbatim #3: Difficult Conversation
description: Submit a verbatim from an encounter involving difficult content (news of a poor prognosis, family conflict, or spiritual distress). In your reflection, identify one thing you would do differently and why.

title: Unit 1 Final: Supervisor Observation - Graded Visit
description: Schedule your graded supervised visit with Rebecca Price no later than the posted due date for this assignment. Verbatim and supervisor evaluation form must be submitted within 48 hours of the visit. APC competency areas 1, 2, and 4 will be assessed.

title: Verbatim #4: Oncology Encounter
description: Write a verbatim from a visit with an oncology patient or family. In your reflection, address the question: What does hope look like in this context? Minimum 150 words in the reflection section.

title: Module 7 Quiz: ICU Chaplaincy Principles
description: Describe two specific challenges unique to chaplaincy in the ICU setting and explain one concrete strategy for addressing each. Your answer should reflect the Module 7 readings and your clinical observation.

title: Palliative Care Reflection: Goals of Care Conversation
description: Observe or participate in a goals-of-care conversation (with attending physician or palliative team). Write a 400-word reflection on the chaplain's role in that conversation. What did you notice? What was your internal experience?

title: Family Systems Assessment: Case Study
description: Using a de-identified patient scenario provided by your supervisor, complete a family systems assessment using the genogram format. Identify the presenting spiritual need, power dynamics, and at least one family strength you would build on.

title: Grief Ministry Plan: Community Resource Map
description: Develop a grief ministry resource map for Bluff City Medical Center. Identify at least 5 community resources (support groups, counseling, faith communities) available to bereaved families in the Memphis area. Provide contact information and a brief description of each.

title: Unit 2 Verbatim: End-of-Life Encounter
description: Submit a verbatim from an end-of-life or actively dying patient encounter. Reflection must address: (1) your theological framework for death and dying; (2) how you maintained your presence amid your own discomfort.

title: Unit 2 Competency Portfolio
description: Compile your Unit 2 portfolio: oncology verbatim, palliative reflection, family systems case study, grief resource map, and end-of-life verbatim with supervisor feedback incorporated. Include an integrative 300-word closing essay on your growth as a specialized chaplain.

title: Guest Booking Template: Outreach Email Draft
description: Draft a guest booking outreach email for one potential guest for Morning Reflections with Rebecca. Guest must align with the show's format (faith, community, inspiration). Include pitch, logistics, and three sample interview questions.

title: Show Format Quiz: Segment Structure
description: Describe the standard two-hour format for Morning Reflections with Rebecca - segment order, length, and purpose of each block. Include how sponsor underwriting reads are positioned relative to content segments.

title: Devotional Script: Original 4-Minute Opening
description: Write an original 4-minute opening devotional script suitable for the Morning Reflections opening segment. Must include a scripture reference, a personal or community story, and a takeaway phrase that could anchor the episode. Aim for warm, grounded delivery - no preamble.

title: Sponsor Underwriting Read: Covenant Insurance Group
description: Write a 30-second underwriting read for Covenant Insurance Group suitable for broadcast. The read must comply with FCC underwriting rules (no calls to action, no pricing), reflect the sponsor's focus on life and auto insurance, and match the show's tone.

title: Audacity Basics: Edit and Export a 90-Second Clip
description: Using Audacity, trim and export a 90-second audio clip from the provided raw recording (posted in Materials). Apply noise reduction, normalize to -3 dB, and export as MP3 320kbps. Submit the exported file and a screenshot of your Audacity session.

title: Fall 2025 Verbatim #1: Initial Clinical Encounter
description: Submit your first clinical verbatim from the Fall 2025 CPE unit. Follow the standard verbatim template. Reflection should address your emotional and spiritual state before, during, and after the encounter.

title: Unit 1 Reading: Foundations of Lay Pastoral Care
description: Read chapters 1-3 of 'The Art of Helping People Effectively' and write a 200-word response: What is one thing you learned that changes how you will listen to someone in your congregation who is hurting?

title: Hospital Visitation Role-Play: Reflection
description: Complete the hospital visitation role-play exercise in pairs during our October 5 session. Afterward, submit a 150-word reflection: What felt natural? What felt uncomfortable? What would you do differently next time?

title: Grief Support: When to Refer Quiz
description: In your own words, describe two signs that a grieving person in your congregation may need professional counseling beyond what a lay care visitor can provide. What would you say to gently encourage a referral?

title: Boundaries Practice Plan
description: Write a personal boundaries plan for your lay care ministry: (1) one situation where you would involve leadership, (2) how you will protect your own spiritual health, (3) what you will say if someone shares something that requires mandatory reporting. Minimum 200 words.
